steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: A participial phrase “Having 9,721 feet of prominence,” is followed by a blank space that must be filled with an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the dangling modifier rule
steps.content: A participial phrase must be immediately followed by the noun it logically modifies. Choice A correctly places “Pico do Ramelau” (the mountain with prominence) as this noun. B, C, and D violate this rule by positioning nouns (the list, geographers, the ranking) that cannot logically possess prominence. That matches choice A — A is correct.
